summary: The CI test run failed due to a single failing fourslash test where the expected code-fix title does not match the actual title produced.
root_cause: A fourslash test assertion failed in tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts because the actual code-fix title 'Add return type 'Foo<string>'' did not match the expected title 'Add return type 'Foo<string, string[…'.

evidence.reason: Shows there was exactly one test failure in the run.
Identifies the failing test file reported by the harness.

evidence.quote: AssertionError: expected 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' to equal 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…'
evidence.reason: The assertion message shows the exact mismatch between the actual and expected code-fix title, indicating a test expectation mismatch.
Shows the failure originated from the fourslash verifyCodeFix assertion code in the test harness.

suggested_fix: Either update the expected code-fix title in tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts to match the actual title 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'', or modify the code-fix implementation so it produces the expected full title 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string…\''. Re-run the test locally with the project's test command (e.g. npm test -- --no-lint --grep codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52) to verify the fix.
